findings_closed.reproduced_before_fix: aggregate.py:73 derived quorum from the PROFILE NAME alone. Three mock responses under profile=frontier_council produced frontier_council_quorum=True. Confirmed empirically.
findings_closed.fix: aggregate() now takes execution_mode (default MOCK_INTERNAL — a caller who forgets gets the SAFE answer) and authorization_consumed. frontier_live_quorum_evidence() requires LIVE_EXTERNAL + consumed authorization + 3 distinct providers + 3 distinct member IDs + raw provider bytes + provider identity FROM the response + resolved model FROM the response + one authoritative run ID. Followed by the hard assertion: `if execution_mode != LIVE_EXTERNAL: frontier_council_quorum = False`. Mock adapters now self-declare adapter_kind='mock' and are refused as live evidence, so a mock cannot masquerade as a provider. The old downgrade depended on a mutable string (status=FROZEN_MOCK_ONLY) in a data file; that dependency is gone.

findings_closed.finding: Multiple packages appear READY or authorization-eligible, including TEST.
findings_closed.fix: coordination/council/h1_candidate_registry.json is now the single canonical registry, generated by scripts/council/generate_h1_candidate_registry.py from EVIDENCE INSIDE each package — never from a filename, never last-write-wins. TEST identity is read from the run_id inside the SIGNED provider requests (HELM-H1-TEST-H1-RUN), which lives inside the digest chain and therefore cannot be shed without breaking combined_authorization_sha256. Supersession is a durable, attributable stamp, not a read-time inference. Anything other than exactly one survivor yields EVIDENCE_RECONCILIATION_REQUIRED and NOTHING is eligible.
findings_closed.second_writer_removed: generate_h1b_packet.py no longer stamps validation.json. Two writers of the same truth was the defect; classification now has exactly one owner.

findings_closed.finding: Enforcement relied on BudgetGovernor; production did not bind package/authorization/digest/provider/model; the validator existed only in tests.
findings_closed.fix: scripts/council/h1_authorization.py is PRODUCTION code and is the ONLY validator. Tests import it; no validator is reimplemented in the test module. It binds: authorization_id, package_id, combined_authorization_sha256, prompt digest, roster digest, contract digest, model-policy digest, budget digest, all three provider-request digests, exact providers, exact models (compared against the models inside the signed requests, not against the authorization's own claims), permitted_run_count, issued_at, expires_at, founder identity, approval reference, operator-hold override scope, and the three substitution/promotion flags. Every digest is RECOMPUTED from disk; nothing is trusted as stored.

findings_closed.finding: No atomic authorization consumption; no durable replay ledger.
findings_closed.fix: coordination/council/authorization_ledger.jsonl — append-only JSONL. AuthorizationLedger.consume() takes an O_CREAT|O_EXCL lock plus fcntl.flock, re-reads the ledger INSIDE the lock (the atomicity boundary), and records authorization_id, package_id, process_id, process_start_time, consumed_at, run_id, request_digest, status. Stale locks are detected by (pid, start_time) so a reused PID cannot impersonate a live holder. authorize_and_consume() validates FULLY and raises BEFORE the ledger is touched, so a rejected authorization is never consumed. A consumed authorization can never be un-consumed: prior bytes are immutable.
findings_closed.concurrency_proof: test_14 spawns two real processes racing the same authorization: exactly one CONSUMED, exactly one BLOCKED, exactly one ledger entry.

findings_closed.fix: Eight INDEPENDENT gates, each bound to its own backend field and colored by its OWN value: h1_package_state, h1_package_integrity, h1_credential_state, h1_founder_authorization (now including CONSUMED, read from the durable ledger), h1_live_provider_proof (NOT_RUN | PARTIAL | PASS | FAILED), h1_frontier_live_quorum, h1_promotion, h1_safe_to_execute. No gate is derived from another. The merged 'READY_FOR_FOUNDER_REVIEW' badge is deleted.

findings_closed.root_cause_found: The frontier guard read frontier_seat_contracts.json from scripts/council/ — a path that DOES NOT EXIST (the real file is in coordination/council/). The defense worked by accident of a typo, not by design.
findings_closed.fix: Path corrected, and the guard now fails CLOSED on an unreadable contract. Independently, every outbound call funnels through _http(), which raises ExternalEgressBlocked for any non-loopback host unless an egress ticket is open. A ticket can only be opened by open_external_egress(), which demands a fully validated AND atomically consumed founder authorization — and which raises unconditionally today because dispatch_live_permitted() is hard-false. Local Ollama is loopback, so the local_proof profile is unaffected: local networking stays isolated to the local profile.

findings_closed.finding: No external paid calls fireable; no live secrets in artifacts.
findings_closed.note: Still true after remediation. Credential checks use `in os.environ` (existence only) — never .get(), which would materialize the value. test_24 scans every committed council artifact for bearer-token-shaped values.
